revision.comment: [[WP:AES|←]]新しいページ: '{{buddhism}} '''水瓶'''（すいびょう）は、[[仏教]]において[[閼伽]]（水）を入れる[[瓶]]で、[[比丘]]（びく）が持たなければ...'
revision.text:
{{buddhism}} '''水瓶'''（すいびょう）は、[[仏教]]において[[閼伽]]（水）を入れる[[瓶]]で、[[比丘]]（びく）が持たなければならない18種類の持ち物のひとつ。[[サンスクリット語]]の「グンディ」の訳で、'''軍持'''（ぐんじ）と音訳される。仏像のうち[[観音菩薩]]などの持物とされることもある。
水瓶は、古くから使われ陶器のほか鋳銅製などがあり、[[文化財]]に指定されているものもある。
==関連項目== *[[瓶]] *[[花瓶]]
[[category:仏教|かひよう]] {{buddhism-stub}}

revision.comment:
Created page with "{{Otheruses|Water Bottles in Buddhism|Water storage jars for daily use|甕}} [[File:荒久遺跡出土 灰釉花文浄瓶.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Gray Glazed Floral Patterned Water Jar
{{small|Excavated from Araku Site (Ichihara City, Chiba Prefecture). Displayed at Ichihara History Museum.}}]] '''Water Jar''' (水瓶, ''suibyō'' or ''mizugame'') is a [[Buddhism|Buddhist]] vessel used to hold [[argha]] (holy water). It is one of the 18 essential possessions that a b..."
revision.text:
{{Otheruses|Water Bottles in Buddhism|Water storage jars for daily use|甕}} [[File:荒久遺跡出土 灰釉花文浄瓶.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Gray Glazed Floral Patterned Water Jar
{{small|Excavated from Araku Site (Ichihara City, Chiba Prefecture). Displayed at Ichihara History Museum.}}]] '''Water Jar''' (水瓶, ''suibyō'' or ''mizugame'') is a [[Buddhism|Buddhist]] vessel used to hold [[argha]] (holy water). It is one of the 18 essential possessions that a [[bhikshu]] (monk) must carry. The Sanskrit term "kundī" is translated as '''Gunji''' (軍持) in phonetic transcription. In Buddhist iconography, it is sometimes depicted as an attribute held by deities such as [[Avalokiteśvara]] (Kannon Bodhisattva). Water jars have been in use since ancient times and can be made of pottery, cast bronze, or other materials. Some examples are designated as [[Cultural Properties]]. == See Also == * [[Jar]] * [[Vase]] * [[Aquarius (constellation)]] * [[Aquarian Age]] {{buddhism2}} [[Category:Buddhist sculptures]] [[Category:Containers]] [[Category:Crafts]] {{buddhism-stub}} [[ja:水瓶]]

In Buddhist iconography, it is sometimes depicted as an attribute held by deities such as [[Avalokiteśvara]] (Kannon Bodhisattva). Water jars have been in use since ancient times and can be made of pottery, cast bronze, or other materials. Some examples are designated as [[Cultural Properties]]. == See Also == * [[Jar]] * [[Vase]] * [[Aquarius (constellation)]] * [[Aquarian Age]] {{buddhism2}} [[Category:Buddhist sculptures]] [[Category:Containers]] [[Category:Crafts]] {{buddhism-stub}} [[Category:Peripheral articles]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:dd]] [[Category:properly attributed pages]][[Category:Kuṇḍikā]][[Category:properly attributed pages]] [[ja:水瓶]]

revision.text:
{{Otheruses|仏教における水瓶|生活用水を蓄える水瓶|甕}} [[ファイル:荒久遺跡出土 灰釉花文浄瓶.JPG|thumb|200px|right|灰釉花文浄瓶
{{small|荒久遺跡（千葉県市原市）出土。市原歴史博物館展示。}}]] '''水瓶'''（すいびょう、みずがめ）は、[[仏教]]において[[閼伽]]（水）を入れる[[瓶]]で、[[比丘]]（びく）が持たなければならない18種類の持ち物のひとつ。[[サンスクリット語]]の「グンディ」の訳で、'''軍持'''（ぐんじ）と音訳される。仏像のうち[[観音菩薩]]などの[[持物]]とされることもある。 水瓶は、古くから使われ陶器のほか鋳銅製などがあり、[[文化財]]に指定されているものもある。 == 関連項目 == * [[瓶]] * [[花瓶]] * [[みずがめ座]] * [[宝瓶宮]] {{buddhism2}} {{DEFAULTSORT:すいひよう}} [[category:仏像]] [[Category:容器]] [[Category:工芸]] {{buddhism-stub}}

It is one of the 18 essential possessions that a [[:en:bhikshu|bhikshu]] (monk) must carry. The Sanskrit term "kundī" is translated as '''Gunji''' (軍持) in phonetic transcription. In Buddhist iconography, it is sometimes depicted as an attribute held by deities such as [[:en:Avalokiteśvara|Avalokiteśvara]] (Kannon Bodhisattva). Water jars have been in use since ancient times and can be made of pottery, cast bronze, or other materials. Some examples are designated as [[Cultural Properties]]. == See Also == * [[:en:Jar|Jar]] * [[:en:Vase|Vase]] * [[:en:Aquarius (constellation)|Aquarius (constellation)]] * [[:en:Aquarian Age|Aquarian Age]] {{buddhism2}} {{Buddhism-stub}} {{translated page|ja|水瓶|version=93862087|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:水瓶]] {{Commons category|Kundikas}} [[zh:軍持]] [[zh-yue:軍持]] {{wikidata link|Q11548974}} [[Category:1000 shortest pages as of Nov 16, 2025]] [[Category:Buddha statues]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:Containers]] [[Category:Crafts]] [[Category:Translated pages on the short pages]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]
